notes: Clean single-weight ink with FAO-like restraint, but the silhouette flattens into a manta/fish shape and the arm crown is collapsed so it does not read as this specific Loligo.
specific_issues: mantle+fin outline merged into a single manta-like wing instead of the photo's rhomboidal fin lobes flanking a tubular mantle
arm crown collapsed — cannot resolve 8 distinct arms with the fanned arrangement seen in the photo
long tentacles present but upper tentacle routes through the head/mantle region rather than arcing cleanly above it as in the photo
stipple scattered uniformly along the contour rather than concentrated in the dense chromatophore band running along the dorsal mantle
ring suckers only sporadically indicated on a couple of arms; absent on most appendages
small extraneous floating dots and a stray rectangle near the head read as trace noise, not scientific stipple
head/mantle proportion off — head region appears too small and lobed compared to the photo
